posts.title: Anthropic Surpasses OpenAI in Valuation at $965B
posts.excerpt: Anthropic raised $65B in a Series H round led by Altimeter, Sequoia, Dragoneer, and Greenoaks, reaching a $965B post-money valuation vs. OpenAI's $730B. Annualized revenue crossed $47B. For…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Anthropic raised $65B in a Series H round led by Altimeter, Sequoia, Dragoneer, and Greenoaks, reaching a $965B post-money valuation vs. OpenAI's $730B. Annualized revenue crossed $47B. For engineers: this signals continued heavy investment in Claude model improvements, safety research, and enterprise infrastructure — Anthropic is now the better-capitalized lab, which has real implications for model quality and API reliability going forward.

---
*Sources: [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/28/anthropic-open-ai-startup-value.html) · [Axios](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/28/anthropic-ai-fundraising-openai) · [Hacker News thread](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48336233)*

posts.title: Microsoft Build 2026: GitHub Copilot Drops GPT-4 for In-House Project Polaris
posts.excerpt: Starting August 2026, GitHub Copilot's default engine switches from GPT-4 Turbo to Project Polaris, Microsoft's own in-house coding model. This ends Copilot's dependence on OpenAI and gives Microsoft…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Starting August 2026, GitHub Copilot's default engine switches from GPT-4 Turbo to Project Polaris, Microsoft's own in-house coding model. This ends Copilot's dependence on OpenAI and gives Microsoft full control over the model roadmap. Separately, the Agent Framework for .NET and Python hit production-ready 1.0 and is now Microsoft's official standard for multi-agent systems on Azure.

---
*Sources: [TechTimes](https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317596/20260602/github-copilot-replaces-gpt-4-project-polaris-ships-multi-agent-vs-code-build.htm) · [Engadget Build 2026 Live Blog](https://www.engadget.com/2185601/microsoft-build-2026-live-blog-copilot-windows-news/)*

posts.title: Google Releases Gemini 3.5 Flash as GA — Benchmarks Beat Gemini 3.1 Pro on Coding
posts.excerpt: Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available at $1.50/$9 per 1M tokens (input/output), 1M token context, and 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 — outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on both coding and agentic…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available at $1.50/$9 per 1M tokens (input/output), 1M token context, and 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 — outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on both coding and agentic benchmarks at 4x the speed. Google also launched WebMCP, a proposed open standard allowing developers to expose structured tools for browser-based AI agents, with a Chrome 149 origin trial starting now.

---
*Sources: [Google I/O 2026 Developer Keynote](https://developers.googleblog.com/all-the-news-from-the-google-io-2026-developer-keynote/) · [9to5Google I/O 2026 Recap](https://9to5google.com/2026/05/19/google-io-2026-news/)*

posts.title: Microsoft Is Pulling Claude Code Licenses — AI Tools Consolidation Is Here
posts.excerpt: Microsoft is canceling Claude Code licenses across its Experiences + Devices division by June 30, steering engineers toward GitHub Copilot CLI. Separately, Uber exhausted its entire 2026 AI coding…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Microsoft is canceling Claude Code licenses across its Experiences + Devices division by June 30, steering engineers toward GitHub Copilot CLI. Separately, Uber exhausted its entire 2026 AI coding tools budget in four months. This is the first major signal of enterprise AI tool consolidation: companies are picking a primary agentic coding tool rather than stacking multiple subscriptions. Engineers should expect their orgs to standardize on one platform in H2 2026.

---
*Sources: [Developer Tech](https://www.developer-tech.com/news/microsoft-claude-code-github-copilot-cli/) · [Windows Forum](https://windowsforum.com/threads/microsoft-cuts-claude-code-pushes-copilot-cli-ai-coding-market-shifts-to-workflow-control.421281/) · [Space Daily](https://spacedaily.com/n-microsoft-is-canceling-claude-code-licenses-across-its-experiences-devices-division-by-june-30-steering-thousands-of-engineers-toward-github-copilot-while-uber-burned-through-its-entire-2026-ai-bu/)*

posts.title: AI Coding Tool Market Share Update: Copilot 51%, Cursor 18%, Claude Code 10%
posts.excerpt: The 2026 Stack Overflow Developer Survey shows GitHub Copilot's professional developer share dropped from 67% to 51%, while Cursor debuted at 18% and Claude Code first appeared at 10%. The market is…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The 2026 Stack Overflow Developer Survey shows GitHub Copilot's professional developer share dropped from 67% to 51%, while Cursor debuted at 18% and Claude Code first appeared at 10%. The market is fragmenting: Copilot holds enterprise, Cursor leads IDE-native adoption, Claude Code becomes the standard for advanced agentic workflows. For individual engineers, the choice increasingly depends on workflow type, not just model quality.

---
*Sources: [Lushbinary – AI Coding Agents Comparison 2026](https://lushbinary.com/blog/ai-coding-agents-comparison-cursor-windsurf-claude-copilot-kiro-2026/) · [Pasquale Pillitteri](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/3392/github-copilot-cursor-claude-code-ai-coding-showdown-2026)*

posts.title: Escape Claude & Cursor Limits: Free Local AI Coding with Ollama + Continue.dev
posts.excerpt: A developer guide for setting up a fully offline, free AI coding environment in VS Code. Install Ollama, pull qwen2.5-coder:7b (daily driver) and qwen2.5-coder:1.5b (autocomplete), then configure…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
A developer guide for setting up a fully offline, free AI coding environment in VS Code. Install Ollama, pull `qwen2.5-coder:7b` (daily driver) and `qwen2.5-coder:1.5b` (autocomplete), then configure Continue.dev to point at `localhost:11434`. Works on M1 MacBook with 8GB RAM; covers 60–70% of typical coding tasks without subscription costs or usage caps.

---
*Sources: [DEV Community](https://dev.to/david_bilsonn/how-i-escaped-claude-cursor-limits-the-ultimate-free-local-ai-coding-setup-with-ollama--2nib)*

posts.title: Addy Osmani's LLM Coding Workflow for 2026
posts.excerpt: Detailed, opinionated workflow from a Google engineer: draft a spec.md before any code, break implementation into file-level tasks, context-pack the model with relevant files (tools like gitingest…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Detailed, opinionated workflow from a Google engineer: draft a `spec.md` *before* any code, break implementation into file-level tasks, context-pack the model with relevant files (tools like `gitingest` help), commit after each chunk as a checkpoint, and maintain a `CLAUDE.md` rules file to steer model behavior to your team's coding style. The core principle: AI is your pair programmer, not your replacement — you stay the director.

---
*Sources: [Addy Osmani's Blog](https://addyosmani.com/blog/ai-coding-workflow/)*

posts.title: Agentic Coding: Delegate, Review, Own
posts.excerpt: The emerging consensus workflow for 2026: AI agents handle first-pass execution, scaffolding, implementation, and tests; engineers review for correctness, risk, and alignment; humans own…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The emerging consensus workflow for 2026: AI agents handle first-pass execution, scaffolding, implementation, and tests; engineers review for correctness, risk, and alignment; humans own architecture, trade-offs, and outcomes. Strict CI gates on PRs (tests, linters, security scans) are essential — they catch issues the agent won't surface on its own. Fast feedback loops (quick compile + test cycles) are what make agents productive vs. unruly.

---
*Sources: [Kilo.ai: Beyond Autocomplete](https://kilo.ai/articles/beyond-autocomplete) · [CodeScene: Agentic Best Practices](https://codescene.com/blog/agentic-ai-coding-best-practice-patterns-for-speed-with-quality)*

posts.title: Build CLAUDE.md (and GEMINI.md) Rules Files for Your Projects
posts.excerpt: One of the highest-ROI things you can do: create a CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) in your repo root with coding style rules, patterns to avoid, preferred libraries, and lint requirements. When you load…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
One of the highest-ROI things you can do: create a `CLAUDE.md` (or equivalent) in your repo root with coding style rules, patterns to avoid, preferred libraries, and lint requirements. When you load this at the start of each session, the model adheres to your conventions instead of defaulting to generic patterns. Copilot and Cursor both support similar project-level instruction files.

---
*Sources: [Addy Osmani's Blog](https://addyosmani.com/blog/ai-coding-workflow/) · [Stack Overflow Blog: Coding Guidelines for AI](https://stackoverflow.blog/2026/03/26/coding-guidelines-for-ai-agents-and-people-too/)*

posts.title: Context Engineering Has Replaced Prompt Engineering
posts.excerpt: The industry has shifted from crafting clever instructions ("prompt engineering") to architecting what information the model operates on ("context engineering"). Think of LLMs as a CPU and the…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The industry has shifted from crafting clever instructions ("prompt engineering") to architecting *what information the model operates on* ("context engineering"). Think of LLMs as a CPU and the context window as RAM — your job is the OS, loading the right code and data for each task. 65% of developers now cite missing or stale context as the leading cause of poor AI code quality, not model capability or prompt phrasing. LangChain's four context strategies (write, select/RAG, compress, isolate) are the practical framework to know.

---
*Sources: [Medium: Context Engineering Has Eaten Prompt Engineering](https://medium.com/@codewithrashid/context-engineering-has-eaten-prompt-engineerin-eb3a869c0362) · [Neo4j Blog](https://neo4j.com/blog/agentic-ai/context-engineering-vs-prompt-engineering/) · [Firecrawl: Context Engineering](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/context-engineering)*
